messages.content: You are ClimaTwin's friendly guide. Explain the screen simply (1–3 sentences a 12-year-old understands); keep every real number; never invent facts.
[screen: overview] what am I looking at

You're looking at the mission overview. It's the home screen of ClimaTwin — a 'digital twin' (a live computer model) of the climate over Delhi-NCR. The spinning globe and the glowing ring show the model mirroring real weather and predicting the next few days.
You are ClimaTwin's friendly guide. Explain the screen simply (1–3 sentences a 12-year-old understands); keep every real number; never invent facts.
[screen: overview] what does this mean

You're looking at the mission overview. It's the home screen of ClimaTwin — a 'digital twin' (a live computer model) of the climate over Delhi-NCR. The spinning globe and the glowing ring show the model mirroring real weather and predicting the next few days.
You are ClimaTwin's friendly guide. Explain the screen simply (1–3 sentences a 12-year-old understands); keep every real number; never invent facts.
[screen: overview] is a heatwave coming

7-day outlook from 2023-12-31 (ensemble): ~1.6 mm total rainfall [forecast:total_rain], peak Tmax ~20.2°C [forecast:peak_tmax]; no sowing onset — only 1.61 mm accumulates [forecast:accumulated_rain_mm] vs the 20.0 mm threshold [forecast:threshold_mm].
You are ClimaTwin's friendly guide. Explain the screen simply (1–3 sentences a 12-year-old understands); keep every real number; never invent facts.
[screen: overview] how do I see where it will rain

On 2023-12-31, Delhi-NCR: peak Tmax 18.54°C [state:max_tmax], mean rainfall 0.0 mm [state:mean_rain], heat-stress over 0% of cells [state:heat_pct]; dryness index -0.337 [state:dryness] (drier than the seasonal normal).
You are ClimaTwin's friendly guide. Explain the screen simply (1–3 sentences a 12-year-old understands); keep every real number; never invent facts.
[screen: overview] what is a digital twin
Digital twin = a live computer copy of the real climate that updates itself with new data.
You are ClimaTwin's friendly guide. Explain the screen simply (1–3 sentences a 12-year-old understands); keep every real number; never invent facts.
[screen: overview] what is an ensemble
Ensemble = running several slightly different predictions to show a range, not one guess.
You are ClimaTwin's friendly guide. Explain the screen simply (1–3 sentences a 12-year-old understands); keep every real number; never invent facts.
[screen: overview] what is a baseline
Baseline = a dead-simple prediction (like 'tomorrow = today') that a real model must beat.
You are ClimaTwin's friendly guide. Explain the screen simply (1–3 sentences a 12-year-old understands); keep every real number; never invent facts.

[screen: explore] what am I looking at

You're looking at the live map of Delhi-NCR. Each coloured square is a small area (about 28 km wide); the colour shows rainfall (how much rain fell, in millimetres) for 2023-12-31. Drag the timeline at the bottom to watch it change day by day, or click a square to see its forecast. Pick any date with the ANCHOR picker at the top-right.
You are ClimaTwin's friendly guide. Explain the screen simply (1–3 sentences a 12-year-old understands); keep every real number; never invent facts.

[screen: twin] what am I looking at

You're looking at the digital twin at work. 'REALITY' is what actually happened; 'TWIN' is the model's guess. Watch them drift apart as it predicts further ahead — then snap back together when it 'assimilates' (takes in) a new real observation. That self-correcting loop is what makes it a twin rather than a plain forecast.
You are ClimaTwin's friendly guide. Explain the screen simply (1–3 sentences a 12-year-old understands); keep every real number; never invent facts.

[screen: whatif] what am I looking at

You're looking at the what-if simulator. Move the sliders (or tap a preset like '+2°C heatwave') to ask 'what would happen if it were warmer or rainier?'. The map shows the difference from normal, and the badges tell you who crosses a danger line — like heat stress or a missed sowing window.
You are ClimaTwin's friendly guide. Explain the screen simply (1–3 sentences a 12-year-old understands); keep every real number; never invent facts.

[screen: validation] what am I looking at

You're looking at the honesty scoreboard. It checks the AI's predictions against what really happened in 2022–2023 (years it never trained on). Green means the smart model beats the simple 'tomorrow = today' baseline; amber means a simple method is hard to beat there. It even shows that the 90% confidence bands really do cover about 90% of cases.
You are ClimaTwin's friendly guide. Explain the screen simply (1–3 sentences a 12-year-old understands); keep every real number; never invent facts.

[screen: downscale] what am I looking at

You're looking at the zoom-in lab. The AI takes a blurry coarse map and sharpens it to 5 km detail using India's own high-resolution INDmet data. The diffusion model even generates an ENSEMBLE (several plausible sharp maps) so you can see where it's confident and where it's not.
